system_description.overview: Chicory AI is an AI agent platform for enterprise data teams. The platform leverages AI to automate data cataloging, data mapping, data debugging, and managing data and ML pipelines. It helps companies manage data pipelines, data engineering, and data operations.

products.Automated Data Understanding Platform: AI-generated unified data ontology from structured and unstructured sources; integration capabilities with client's existing technology stack; high dimensional graph relationships between data, code, and documents.
products.AI Agent Development Features: Automated context engineering; simple programming of agent inputs and outputs; deployment environment to host AI agents as APIs; workflow creation from runbook and playbook documents; runtime data validation to prevent hallucinations.
products.Implementation and Support Services: Onboarding, setup, AI system design, technical support, and building new connectors.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — AWS is a subservice organization; AWS controls excluded from scope)
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers. All data processed on cloud services.
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (multiple availability zones in AWS; DR procedure spins up multiple servers across all AZs)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS. Intrusion detection system monitors and detects unauthorized access or anomalies within the network.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN.
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from established third-party certificate authority.
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet via proxy.
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic -> Load Balancer -> WebApp -> Backend API -> Workers; DocumentDB in Security Group within VPC. Bastion host for remote access.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (WebApp + Backend API + Workers architecture visible in network diagram)
application_architecture.api_style: REST (API hosting mentioned; agents deployed as APIs)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments. Changes tested in dev/test before production promotion.

databases: DocumentDB (Amazon DocumentDB visible in network diagram)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (backups taken daily; multi-AZ configured; alerts for backup failures)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM (password policy via AWS; Active Directory mentioned for infrastructure accounts)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform. MFA required for source code tool access. Two-factor authentication required for email.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS password policy; periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited failed logon attempts.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool referenced; MFA protected)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge requests to master branch require additional approval. Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request requires additional approval before merging to production.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configuration implementation and rollback. Baseline configurations retained as code in infrastructure.
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO. Monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tool with threshold-based alerting described)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configurations, permissions, and roles. Log access is restricted.
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events. Change-management/ticketing application used for tracking.

third_party_services.purpose: Data center and cloud infrastructure services (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection software on all production servers (continuous scanning; weekly definition updates) and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Email scanning for malicious scripts and viruses.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications. Internal IT Infrastructure team performs periodic scans.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party.

untestable: CC2.3 - No significant personnel/role changes made during engagement period (could not test internal communication of changes)
CC3.2 - Cybersecurity insurance control untestable because no cyber incidents occurred during period
CC6.5 / CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion untestable because no customer terminations occurred during period
CC7.3.1 - Security incident response control untestable because no security incidents reported during period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to data center facilities excluded; AWS is responsible for physical security
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded; Chicory does not have an office; managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust service categories determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) established with Board oversight; meets semiannually
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) meets weekly to review security activities
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Vendor management with quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors lacking SOC reports
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios

infrastructure.rationale: AWS-hosted, VPC with segmentation, multi-AZ failover, bastion host, IDS/IPS. No CDN mentioned, no multi-region, no named IaC tool (configuration-as-code referenced but unnamed). Single cloud provider.

application_architecture.rationale: Clear Load Balancer -> WebApp -> Backend API -> Workers pattern visible; separate environments; branch protection enforced. Languages/frameworks not disclosed. No container orchestration mentioned.

data_layer.rationale: DocumentDB identified, daily backups confirmed, multi-AZ, data classification and retention policies in place, per-tenant segregation confirmed. No caching layer, no data warehouse, limited storage detail.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS via AWS, firewalls, antivirus on servers and endpoints, MFA enforced, encryption at rest and in transit, cybersecurity insurance. No WAF explicitly named, no SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection enforced, separate environments, emergency change process, configuration-as-code for baselines, automated patch management. CI/CD tool and source control platform not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, reviewed and tested annually, daily backups, multi-AZ failover, multi-scenario DR testing. No stated RTO/RPO, no multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Single subservice organization (AWS) identified in scope. No other named third-party vendors. Very high AWS concentration risk.

overall.rationale: Chicory AI demonstrates a solid security posture for an early-stage AI platform: multi-AZ AWS infrastructure, MFA, quarterly access reviews, annual pen testing, monthly vuln scans, BCDR testing, and cybersecurity insurance. Key gaps are absence of named CI/CD tooling, no WAF or SIEM, no RTO/RPO disclosure, no multi-region DR, and near-total AWS concentration.

red_flags: Extreme AWS concentration — entire infrastructure is single-cloud AWS; no named secondary provider or fallback
No RTO or RPO disclosed despite BCDR policy existing
WAF not mentioned anywhere in the report
No SIEM or named log aggregation tool
Source control and CI/CD tools not named, making toolchain assessment impossible
yellow_flags: No CDN mentioned — potential performance/availability risk for distributed enterprise customers
No multi-region failover — multi-AZ only; regional AWS outage is a single point of failure
DocumentDB is the only database mentioned; unclear if separate transactional and operational stores exist
No programming languages or frameworks disclosed — limits code security assessment
Availability and Confidentiality trust criteria listed but audit scope headline says Security only
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
MFA enforced for all admin access and source code tool access
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) with Board oversight — mature governance for an early-stage company
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and failure alerting
Separate development/test and production environments with enforced branch protection
Anti-virus on production servers with weekly definition updates
key_observations: The product system title is 'The First AI Agent Platform for Enterprise Data Teams' — product positioning is agentic AI for data operations, not general automation
DocumentDB (document-store) aligns with the product's graph-relationship data model for unified data ontology
RGEC and ITLC governance structures are unusually mature for a company of this apparent size (CEO + President + CTO org chart)
The carve-out for AWS means all physical, environmental, and data center controls are outside the audit scope — buyers should obtain AWS SOC 2 report separately
No significant system changes during the audit period — limits some control testability but suggests platform stability
Cybersecurity insurance is explicitly tested as a control (CC3.2) — notable proactive risk transfer mechanism
